content.problemStatement:
There are NNN subatomic particles lined up in a row. There are two types: protons and electrons. Protons have a positive charge and are represented by 111, while electrons have a negative charge and are represented by 000.

Our current understanding of physics gives us a way to predict how the particles will be spaced out, if we know their charges. Two adjacent particles will be separated by 111 unit if they have opposite charges, and 222 units if they have the same charge.

When Chef is not in the kitchen, he is doing physics experiments on subatomic particles. He is testing the hypothesis by having NNN particles in a row, and he will change the charge of a particle KKK times. In the iii-th update, he will change the charge of the QiQ_iQi​-th particle. After each update, find the distance between the first and last particle.

**Note:** Each update is persistent for further updates.
constraints: The sum of KKK over all testcases is at most 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains three lines of input.
The first line contains two integers NNN, KKK.
The second line contains a string SSS of length NNN, where SiS_iSi​ represents the initial charge on iii-th particle.
The third line contains KKK integers Q1,Q2,…,QKQ_1, Q_2, \ldots, Q_KQ1​,Q2​,…,QK​, the positions of the changed particles.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output KKK lines, where the iii-th line contains the answer after the updates Q1,…,QiQ_1,\ldots, Q_iQ1​,…,Qi​ have been made.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Update 1: After reversing the parity of particle 222, the new configuration is 000000000. Since all the particles have a similar charge, each is separated from the previous by a distance of 222 units. So the location of particle 333 is 2+2=42 + 2 = 42+2=4 units from the first particle.
Update 2: After reversing the parity of particle 111, the new configuration is 100100100. Here, the charges of particles 111 and 222 differ, so they are separated by 111 unit. The charges of particles 222 and 333 agree, so they are separated by 222 units. So, the location of particle 333 is 1+2=31 + 2 = 31+2=3 units from the first particle.
Update 3: After reversing the charge of particle 333, the new configuration is 101101101. Here, particles 111 and 222 are separated by 111 unit and particles 222 and 333 are separated by 111 unit. So the location of particle 333 is 1+1=21 + 1 = 21+1=2 units from the first particle.
